name: Session Limit Rule
description: Scenario where a business wants to limit concurrent sessions per user to 3.

input.project_context: B2B SaaS with high security requirements. Using Auth0 for authentication.
input.functional_requirement: Limit each user to a maximum of 3 concurrent active sessions to prevent account sharing.
input.risk_factors: Account sharing leads to revenue loss and security exposure. High-profile clients sensitive to data leaks.

thought_process: 1. Risk: Unauthorized session growth and credential sharing. 2. Governance: Security baseline requires explicit limits. 3. Syntax: Policy should check 'session_count' against constant '3'.

trigger_audit_payload.domain_context: Authentication Governance - Risk: Unauthorized session sharing and revenue leakage.

name: Refund Authorization
description: Scenario for financial limits on refund operations.

input.project_context: E-commerce platform backend. Payment gateway integration.
input.functional_requirement: Only users with the 'SupportManager' role can authorize refunds greater than $500.
input.risk_factors: Financial fraud, internal theft, unauthorized large payouts.

thought_process: 1. Risk: Non-managerial users issuing large refunds. 2. Governance: Principle of least privilege for financial transactions. 3. Logic: If amount > 500, check for 'SupportManager' role.

trigger_audit_payload.domain_context: Financial Governance - Risk: Fraud and unauthorized high-value refunds.
policies: permit(principal, action, resource) when { principal.role == 'SupportManager' || resource.amount <= 500 };
